title: Complete DID resolution algorithm with verification methods

artifacts.description: Extend with W3C DID Core §7.1-compliant resolution algorithm: input normalization, IdentityRegistry event query procedure, DID Document construction rules, error codes (invalidDid, notFound, deactivated), and metadata fields (created, updated, versionId)

Standalone resolution algorithm spec: pseudo-code for the full resolve(did, resolutionOptions) → {didDocument, didDocumentMetadata, didResolutionMetadata} flow, covering both agent (EdDSA Baby Jubjub) and human (nullifier-based) DID subjects

Add indexed event fields and a view function getRegistration(bytes32 commitment) returning (keyType, publicKey, merkleRoot, timestamp, active) to support off-chain resolver queries without replaying all logs

Implement did:bolyra resolver: parse DID → extract commitment, call IdentityRegistry view or scan RegistrationRecorded events, construct DID Document with verificationMethod, authentication, assertionMethod, and service arrays

DID Document builder: typed constructors for AgentVerificationMethod (type: JsonWebKey2020 with Baby Jubjub JWK), HumanVerificationMethod (type: BolyraZkpAuthentication2024 with nullifierCommitment), and RegistryServiceEndpoint

Unit + integration tests: resolve known agent DID → verify verificationMethod JWK fields; resolve human DID → verify nullifier-based method; resolve unknown commitment → expect notFound; resolve deactivated DID → expect deactivated metadata flag

Integration guide for resolver library authors: DID syntax ABNF, resolution driver registration snippet (did-resolver npm pattern), RPC provider configuration, caching recommendations, and known limitations (no universal resolver driver submitted yet)

Conformance test vectors: 4 input DIDs (valid agent, valid human, malformed, deactivated) each with expected didDocument snapshot, didResolutionMetadata.error value or null, and didDocumentMetadata fields

steps: Audit current spec/did-method-bolyra.md: identify gaps against W3C DID Core §7.1 (resolve), §7.2 (resolveRepresentation), and §7.3 (dereference) — document each missing clause as a numbered gap
Define the DID syntax extension: confirm did:bolyra:<commitment> where <commitment> is the 32-byte Poseidon commitment hex-encoded; specify normalization rules (lowercase, no leading 0x)

Add getRegistration(bytes32) view function to IdentityRegistry.sol returning all fields needed for DID Document construction; emit RegistrationRecorded and RegistrationRevoked events with indexed commitment field

Define two verification method types: (a) JsonWebKey2020 for agents — crv: Baby Jubjub, x/y coordinates from EdDSA public key; (b) BolyraZkpAuthentication2024 for humans — nullifierCommitment field, proofPurpose: authentication only

Specify DID Document skeleton: @context array (DID Core + Bolyra context URL), id, controller (self), verificationMethod array, authentication array (by reference), assertionMethod array (agents only), service array with BolyraRegistryService endpoint pointing to on-chain registry address + chainId

Write resolution algorithm pseudo-code: (1) parse and validate DID string, (2) extract commitment bytes, (3) query IdentityRegistry.getRegistration(), (4) if not found return notFound error, (5) if revoked return deactivated + didDocumentMetadata.deactivated=true, (6) determine subject type (agent vs human) from keyType field, (7) construct verificationMethod entry, (8) assemble full DID Document, (9) populate didDocumentMetadata with created/updated timestamps from event block timestamps, (10) return resolution result
Implement sdk/src/resolver.ts following the pseudo-code; expose resolve(did, options) matching did-resolver npm interface so it plugs into Veramo, DIF Universal Resolver driver pattern
Implement sdk/src/didDocument.ts typed builders; validate Baby Jubjub JWK format against draft-ietf-cose-bls-key-representations conventions
Write conformance test vectors in spec/conformance/did-resolution-vectors.json covering all four cases (valid agent, valid human, malformed syntax, deactivated)

Write sdk/test/resolver.test.ts against a Hardhat in-process node: deploy IdentityRegistry, register agent and human entries, run resolve() calls, assert DID Document shape and metadata fields against vectors

Update spec/did-method-bolyra.md: insert §5 Resolution Algorithm (normative), §6 Verification Methods (normative), §7 Service Endpoints (normative), §8 Security Considerations additions (nullifier linkability, key rotation not supported in v1)
Write docs/did-resolver-integration.md with copy-paste driver registration snippet and RPC configuration examples for Base Sepolia and Base Mainnet
dependencies: contracts/src/IdentityRegistry.sol — must expose getRegistration() view and indexed events before resolver can be implemented
did-resolver npm package (Veramo ecosystem interface — Resolver class, DIDResolutionResult type)
Baby Jubjub JWK encoding — draft-ietf-cose-bls-key-representations or iden3 JWK convention (must pick one and document it)
Poseidon hash parameters used in commitment derivation — must be pinned so resolvers can re-derive and verify commitment == hash(publicKey, salt)
Base Sepolia RPC endpoint and deployed IdentityRegistry contract address (needed for integration test and docs)
spec/draft-bolyra-mutual-zkp-auth-01.md — resolution algorithm must be consistent with handshake nonce binding described there
